PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dennis Vann for and with Paula Bibbs. Joseph Barton for Barbara DeVries.

The Court stated that there was a petition from Rodney Smith, one from Barbara DeVries and one from Paula Bibbs. The Court informed Mr. Vann that there was no Proof of Service or Proof of Publication on his petition, stated that Ms. DeVries requested full authority with a $240,000.00 bond, that if her petition was granted that it would have to be with limited authority and with full bond and that there was a discrepancy with value between Rodney and Ms. DeVries. Mr. Barton agreed with limited authority, stated that Rodney based values on online comps and that Ms. DeVries values were based on her Son being a professional realtor. Mr. Vann stated that he had no time to do publication, that he received Rodney's objections yesterday, that there were serious problems with the Estate, that there was a lot of work to be done, that cooperation of Rodney and Reginald was needed and that they should both serve so that all Family members feel that they are being represented. The Court stated that it read Judge Feinstein's statement of decision, and that both Ms. DeVries and Ms. Bibbs were qualified. The Court granted the petition for appointment of Barbara DeVries with limited authority and with bond in the amount of $240,000.00 (subject to a written order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.
